content:
Farin Kamangar, MD, PhD, CRA is the Associate Vice President for Research, University Distinguished Professor.

Office: Tyler Hall, Suite 304. Phone: 443-885-3447. Email: farin.kamangar@morgan.edu.

Dr. Farin Kamangar is a University Distinguished Professor and Associate Vice President for Research at Morgan State University. He leads the Office of Research Administration and oversees the University's research strategy and externally sponsored research portfolio.

Dr. Kamangar is a cancer epidemiologist and has published over 280 peer-reviewed articles, many in top-tier journals such as The Lancet, BMJ, Annals of Internal Medicine, Lancet Oncology, Journal of Clinical Oncology, Journal of the National Cancer Institute, and Gastroenterology.

He has served as PI or co-PI on over $60 million of NIH-funded projects, including the ASCEND Training Model to Enhance the Diversity of the Biomedical Research Workforce, as well as major epidemiologic studies including the Golestan Cohort Study — a large cohort of over 50,000 individuals with over 20 years of follow-up.

He is a Fellow of the American Association for the Advancement of Science (AAAS) and has received the NIH Merit Award (group award) and the NCI Director's Innovation Award.

Dr. Kamangar has collaborated with researchers at the NIH, Johns Hopkins, the University of Maryland, the International Agency for Research on Cancer, and Tehran University of Medical Sciences.

He has a passion for teaching and mentoring across all levels — from high school to doctoral — with significant experience mentoring minority students.

Education: PhD in Epidemiology, Johns Hopkins University; MHS in Biostatistics, Johns Hopkins University; MPH, Tehran University of Medical Sciences; MD, Tehran University of Medical Sciences.

Certifications: Certified Research Administrator (CRA) (Research Administrators Certification Council; Certified Financial Research Administrator (CFRA) (Research Administrators Certification Council; Certified Pre-Award Research Administrator (CPRA) (Research Administrators Certification Council.

Certificates: Organizational Leadership (Harvard Business School); Financial Accounting (Harvard Business School); Economics (Harvard Business School).

Research interests: Clinical research; Cancer epidemiology; Cardiovascular disease epidemiology; Clinical trials; Epidemiology of esophageal and gastric cancers; Carcinogenicity of opium use; Exposure to polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons; Consumption of hot beverages; Effects of opium use on cardiovascular diseases.

Awards: Fellow, American Association for the Advancement of Science (AAAS); NIH Merit Award (group award); NCI Director's Innovation Award.

Major grants: ASCEND Training Model to Enhance the Diversity of the Biomedical Research Workforce (NIH); Golestan Cohort Study (>50,000 participants, 20+ years follow-up).
